---
title: "D2.2 — Data Formats and Metadata"
subtitle: "IMBUS Deliverable D2.2 · Work Package 2"
author: "Einar Hjörleifsson (subcontracted to DTU Aqua)"
date: "June 2026"
date-modified: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

> **Project:** IMBUS — Implementing More and Better Use of ICES Survey Data  
> **Deliverable:** D2.2 — Data formats and meta-data  
> **Work Package:** WP2 — User requirements, data formats and meta-data  
> **Status:** DRAFT  
> **Due:** Month 11 (July 2026)

---

## Executive Summary

This document is the formal output of Task T2.3 (Data Formats and Meta-data) of the IMBUS project.
It defines the data format specification and metadata description for a standardised representation
of the ICES DATRAS bottom trawl survey exchange tables — the **HH** (haul header), **HL**
(haul-level catch at length), and **CA** (catch at age) record types.

The scope of D2.2 is the exchange data itself. Per the WP2-WP5 meeting of 1 June 2026, derived
products such as CPUE-by-age indices are outside WP2 scope. The CPUE-by-length calculation
pathway is described (Section 15) as a transparent, reproducible procedure that any user can apply
to the standardised exchange tables, not as a deployed data service.

The principal design decisions are:

1. **Unified column naming.** A single, consistent set of column names (the standard parquet
   names) is applied across all record types and surveys, regardless of API source or era.
2. **Four-layer HL decomposition.** The HL table conflates haul join keys, sampling protocol,
   catch totals, and length frequencies. These are documented as distinct layers, with a proposed
   split into separate parquet files pending final input from ICES and the IMBUS team.
3. **Parquet-based distribution.** HH, HL, and CA are stored as columnar parquet files enabling
   fast, column-selective querying without full downloads.
4. **Sentinel value removal.** The DATRAS convention of using −9 for missing values is replaced
   with proper `NA`.
5. **Species resolution via WoRMS AphiaID.** Historical species codes (ITIS TSN, NODC) are
   mapped to current WoRMS AphiaIDs for cross-era joins.
6. **Unique haul identifiers.** An 8-field composite key (`.id`) uniquely identifies each haul
   across all surveys, years, and countries.

The specification is implemented in the R package **{obus}**
(<https://github.com/einarhjorleifsson/obus>) and accompanied by illustrative code throughout.
Parquet files are currently hosted at <https://heima.hafro.is/~einarhj/datras/> (interim; transfer
to the ICES open access portal is planned under D2.4).

---

## Background and Project Context

The ICES DATRAS database holds fisheries-independent bottom trawl survey data submitted by
national institutes across the Northeast Atlantic, covering surveys from 1965 to the present.
The data are structured as three exchange tables — HH, HL, and CA — which together describe each
trawl haul, its catch by length class, and biological measurements on individual fish.

Despite being publicly accessible, these data have historically been difficult to use. The WP2
user requirements survey (D2.1, April 2026, n = 55 respondents) identified four dominant
challenges:

- Complex table structure (HH/HL/CA hierarchy, join logic)
- Column name changes introduced around 2020 (legacy XML names vs. current parquet names)
- Quality-control inconsistencies
- Slow downloads

The WP2-WP5 alignment meeting (1 June 2026) confirmed that the core objective of WP2 is to
produce a **standardised data format specification and metadata description** that removes hidden
complexity, brings fragmented field-level documentation under one umbrella, and describes the
data flow from DATRAS to end users. This document is that specification.

---

## Scope

### In scope

- The three DATRAS exchange record types: **HH**, **HL**, and **CA**.
- Field-level metadata: names, types, units, descriptions, and known interpretation pitfalls.
- The four-layer decomposition of HL and the proposed split design.
- The DataType field and its arithmetic implications for HL.
- Unique haul identification, species resolution, and length unit standardisation.
- The calculation pathway from raw exchange data to CPUE-by-length (as a documented procedure,
  not a deployed product).
- **Exploratory CPUE-by-age:** The calculation pathway from exchange data to age-disaggregated
  CPUE, using an age-length key (ALK) constructed from the CA table and applied to HL length
  frequencies. This is distinct from the official CPUEA product — it is a transparent,
  user-driven procedure for research and exploration, not a formal assessment input.
- **Proposed intermediate (middle-data) parquet products:** Pre-processed derived tables that
  resolve DataType arithmetic, standardise length units, and separate the conceptual layers of HL
  — removing low-level implementation detail from the user's analytical path.
- Area standardisation: mapping haul positions to survey strata and ICES statistical areas.

### Out of scope

- **Official CPUEA and age-based survey indices:** The ICES Datacenter computes formal
  age-disaggregated CPUE products (CPUEA) and abundance indices (IDX) using survey- and
  species-specific age-slicing procedures agreed by ICES expert groups. Replicating these
  specific products, or replacing the expert-group decisions embedded in them, is not part of WP2.
  These products remain accessible as-is via `dr_get("CPUEA", ...)` and `dr_get("IDX", ...)`.
- **Deployed data service:** WP2 produces a specification and supporting code. Deployment to
  the ICES open access portal is the subject of D2.4.
- **Deployed data service:** WP2 produces a specification and supporting code. Deployment to
  the ICES open access portal is the subject of D2.4.
- **Per-survey metadata fact sheets:** Coverage, gear history, time-series breaks, and QC
  caveats at the survey level are addressed separately under user requirement UR-Q1.

---

## Surveys Covered

The specification applies to all ICES surveys available in DATRAS, excluding surveys flagged as
test surveys. The full survey list is retrieved dynamically via `icesDatras::getSurveyList()`.
Principal surveys include:

| Survey | Area | Season |
|--------|------|--------|
| NS-IBTS | North Sea | Q1, Q3 |
| SWC-IBTS | West of Scotland | Q1, Q4 |
| BTS | Southern North Sea / Eastern Channel | Q3 |
| BITS | Baltic Sea | Q1, Q4 |
| DYFS | Southern North Sea / Eastern Channel | Q3 |
| CGFS | Eastern Channel | Q4 |
| EVHOE | Bay of Biscay | Q4 |
| IGFS | Irish Shelf | Q4 |
| NIGFS | Northern Ireland | Q4 |

Additional surveys (ROCKALL, PORCUPINE, SP-NORTH, SP-ARSA, PT-IBTS, SCOROC, and others) are also
included. Coverage varies by survey, year, and quarter.

---

## Data Flow

The standardisation pipeline has four steps:

```
DATRAS database
      │
      ▼
Raw exchange download    ← getDATRAS() (legacy XML API)
      │
      ▼
Type normalisation       ← dr_settypes(): column types enforced; −9 → NA
Column renaming          ← dr_translate(): legacy → standard names
      │
      ▼
Parquet files (server)   ← HH.parquet / HL.parquet / CA.parquet
      │
      ▼
User pipeline            ← dr_con() / dr_get() → filter → collect()
                         → dr_add_id(), dr_add_length_mm(), dr_add_n_and_cpue(), …
```

The parquet files contain only the exchange table fields with standard names plus `.id`. All
further computation — CPUE arithmetic, zero-filling, spatial joins — is left to user pipelines
or the helper functions described in this document. This ensures that the core data product is
independent of any particular analytical choice.

---

## Column Naming Convention

Two naming conventions exist in DATRAS, reflecting a change introduced by ICES around 2020:

| Convention | Source | Example fields |
|------------|--------|----------------|
| **Legacy** | `getDATRAS()` (XML API); pre-~2020 DATRAS downloads | `Ship`, `HaulNo`, `StNo`, `LngtCode`, `HLNoAtLngt`, `SubFactor`, `TotalNo`, `HaulVal`, `HaulDur` |
| **Standard** | Parquet files; IMBUS product; current ICES convention | `Platform`, `HaulNumber`, `StationName`, `LengthCode`, `NumberAtLength`, `SubsamplingFactor`, `TotalNumber`, `HaulValidity`, `HaulDuration` |

The IMBUS product uses **standard names throughout**. Where data originate from the legacy XML
API (derived products FL, LT, CPUEL, CPUEA, CW, IDX), an automatic translation step maps
legacy to standard names using the field dictionary `dr_lookup_fields`. Fields with no standard
equivalent retain their original names.

The full mapping (~292 fields) is available in the `dr_lookup_fields` data object in {obus}.
Key mappings for HH, HL, and CA are listed in [Annex A].

---

## HH — Haul Header

One row per haul. HH is the parent table; all child tables (HL, CA) join to it on `.id`.

### Haul identification and join key

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `Survey` | chr | Survey acronym (e.g. `"NS-IBTS"`) |
| `Year` | int | Year of survey |
| `Quarter` | int | Quarter (1–4) |
| `Country` | chr | ISO country code of contributing vessel |
| `Platform` | chr | Vessel identifier (SeaDataNet code) |
| `Gear` | chr | Gear code (e.g. `"GOV"`) |
| `StationName` | chr | Station identifier within survey |
| `HaulNumber` | int | Haul sequence number |
| `.id` | chr | Composite haul key (see [Unique Haul Identifier]) |

### Haul outcome

| Field | Type | Unit | Description |
|-------|------|------|-------------|
| `HaulValidity` | chr | — | `"V"` = valid; `"I"` = invalid. Filter to `"V"` for standard analyses |
| `DataType` | chr | — | Sampling protocol (see [The DataType Field]) |
| `HaulDuration` | dbl | Minutes | Duration of tow |
| `ReasonHaulDisruption` | chr | — | Disruption code for invalid hauls |

### Temporal

| Field | Type | Unit | Description |
|-------|------|------|-------------|
| `Month` | int | — | Month of haul |
| `Day` | int | — | Day of haul |
| `StartTime` | chr | HHMM GMT | Start time (`dr_add_starttime()` → POSIXct) |

`dr_add_date()` and `dr_add_starttime()` derive a tidy `date` (Date) and `time` (POSIXct) column
from these fields; the raw fields are then redundant.

### Position and area

| Field | Type | Unit | Description |
|-------|------|------|-------------|
| `ShootLatitude` | dbl | Decimal degrees | Tow start latitude |
| `ShootLongitude` | dbl | Decimal degrees | Tow start longitude |
| `HaulLatitude` | dbl | Decimal degrees | Tow end latitude |
| `HaulLongitude` | dbl | Decimal degrees | Tow end longitude |
| `StatisticalRectangle` | chr | — | ICES statistical rectangle; derivable from position |
| `SurveyIndexArea` | chr | — | Survey-specific stratum used in index calculations |
| `DepthStratum` | chr | — | Survey-specific depth band |
| `DayNight` | chr | — | `"D"` / `"N"` (15 min before/after sunrise/sunset) |

`StatisticalRectangle` and `DayNight` are derivable from position and time; they are retained for
convenience. See [Area Standardisation] for mapping to ICES areas.

### Gear and environmental fields

Key gear fields: `Distance` (metres), `WingSpread` (metres), `DoorSpread` (metres),
`GroundSpeed` (knots), `BottomDepth` (metres).

Key environmental fields: `BottomTemperature` (°C), `SurfaceTemperature` (°C),
`BottomSalinity` (PSU), `SurfaceSalinity` (PSU).

The full set of gear configuration and environmental fields is documented in
`dr_lookup_fields` and in the DATRAS Technical Reference.

---

## HL — Haul-level Length

HL is the most complex exchange table because it conflates four conceptually distinct layers in a
single flat structure. Understanding this structure is essential for correct CPUE arithmetic.

### The four layers {#hl-four-layers}

**Layer A — haul join key** (redundant after joining to HH on `.id`):  
`Survey`, `Year`, `Quarter`, `Country`, `Platform`, `Gear`, `StationName`, `HaulNumber`,
plus `GearExceptions`, `SweepLength`, `DoorType` (duplicated from HH).

**Layer B — sampling protocol** (constant within a species × sex × category group):

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `SpeciesValidity` | chr | Record type — see below |
| `SpeciesCategory` | chr | Subsampling category ID (up to 5 per species/sex/haul; always `"1"` for DataType C) |
| `SubsamplingFactor` | dbl | Raise factor: e.g. 6 means 1/6 of catch was measured |
| `SubsampledNumber` | dbl | Count of fish actually measured in the subsample |
| `SubsampleWeight` | dbl | Weight of the measured subsample (grams) |

> **Note (A2):** `SubsampledNumber` and `SubsampleWeight` are Layer B fields not included in
> the earlier draft of D2.2. Vaishav Soni and Adrianna Villamor have been asked to confirm
> their presence and exact semantics in the exchange data (action point A2, 1 June 2026).

**Layer C — catch totals** (values repeated identically across every length row of the group):

| Field | Type | Unit | Description |
|-------|------|------|-------------|
| `ValidAphiaID` | int | — | Resolved WoRMS AphiaID (see [Species Identification]) |
| `SpeciesSex` | chr | — | Population-level sex code: `"M"` / `"F"` / `"U"` / `NA` |
| `DevelopmentStage` | chr | — | Non-invasive maturity or stage observation |
| `TotalNumber` | dbl | — | Total catch per group; semantics depend on DataType (see [The DataType Field]) |
| `SpeciesCategoryWeight` | dbl | Grams | Total catch weight per group; also DataType-dependent |

> **Note (A2):** `DevelopmentStage` is a Layer C field not covered in the earlier D2.2 draft.
> Its prevalence and the conditions under which it is populated require confirmation.

**Layer D — length frequency** (one row per length class):

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `LengthCode` | chr | Unit flag for `LengthClass` (see [Length Units]) |
| `LengthClass` | int | Length class value (unit set by `LengthCode`) |
| `LengthType` | chr | Measurement type: total length, fork length, mantle length, etc. |
| `NumberAtLength` | dbl | Count in the measured sub-sample at this length class; semantics depend on DataType |

> **Note (A2):** `LengthType` is a Layer D field absent from the earlier D2.2 draft. It is
> particularly relevant for species where standard length type differs by country or era.

### The redundancy problem

Layer C values (`TotalNumber`, `SpeciesCategoryWeight`) are repeated identically across every
length row for a given `.id` × species × sex × category group. A species with 30 length bins
produces 30 copies of the same totals — ~97% redundancy for catch-level fields. This is the root
cause of most HL confusion and the main motivation for the proposed split described below.

### SpeciesValidity — the record-type gatekeeper

`SpeciesValidity` controls what data are present in a row and which rows should be included in
derived products:

| Value | Meaning | Include in products |
|-------|---------|---------------------|
| `"1"` | Full record: lengths, numbers, weights | Yes |
| `"0"` | Invalid | No |
| `"4"` | Total number only; no lengths | Situation-dependent |
| `"5"` | Presence/absence only | Situation-dependent |
| `"6"` | Weight only | Situation-dependent |
| `"7"` | Numbers and weights; no lengths | Situation-dependent |
| `"10"` | Lengths and numbers; no weights | Situation-dependent |

Standard DATRAS products filter to `SpeciesValidity == "1"`. The {obus} functions expose this via
the `specval` argument (default `1L`).

### `DataType` is in HH, not HL

`DataType` describes how the entire haul's catch was processed — it is a property of the haul,
not of any species record. It must be joined from HH before any HL arithmetic:

```r
hl |> left_join(hh |> select(.id, DataType, HaulDuration), by = ".id")
```

### Proposed HL split design {#hl-split}

Given the four-layer structure, the preferred end-state is to split HL into two separate parquet
files. This split requires final input from Vaishav Soni (ICES) and the IMBUS team (action
point A2); the design below is a proposal.

**`HL_catch.parquet`** — one row per `.id` × `ValidAphiaID` × `SpeciesSex` × `DevelopmentStage`
× `SpeciesCategory`. Contains catch totals and subsampling bookkeeping; no length information.

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `.id` | Join key |
| `ValidAphiaID` | Resolved WoRMS AphiaID |
| `SpeciesValidity` | Record type filter |
| `SpeciesSex` | Population-level sex (visual inspection of bulk catch) |
| `DevelopmentStage` | Non-invasive stage observation |
| `SpeciesCategory` | Subsampling category ID |
| `SubsamplingFactor` | Raise factor |
| `SubsampledNumber` | Fish actually measured |
| `SubsampleWeight` | Weight of measured subsample (grams) |
| `n_haul` | DataType-corrected total count per haul (replaces raw `TotalNumber`) |
| `w_haul` | DataType-corrected catch weight per haul in grams (replaces `SpeciesCategoryWeight`) |

**`HL_length.parquet`** — one row per `.id` × `ValidAphiaID` × `SpeciesSex` × `DevelopmentStage`
× `SpeciesCategory` × `length_cm`. Contains length frequency distribution only.

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `.id` | Join key |
| `ValidAphiaID` | Resolved WoRMS AphiaID |
| `SpeciesSex` | |
| `DevelopmentStage` | |
| `SpeciesCategory` | |
| `length_cm` | Standardised length (from `LengthCode` + `LengthClass`) |
| `LengthType` | Fork / total / mantle / etc. |
| `NumberAtLength` | Count in the subsample bin |
| `SubsamplingFactor` | Retained here to avoid a mandatory join for the most common scaling |

Open questions requiring IMBUS team input before the split can be finalised:

- Should `DevelopmentStage` be part of the grouping key, or aggregated away?
- How to handle DataType P (pseudocategory) hauls where the same species appears in multiple
  `SpeciesCategory` strata?
- Should `SpeciesSex` be retained in the split tables or aggregated?
- What is the `SpeciesCategoryWeight` definition precisely when
  `DevelopmentStage` varies within a species/category group?

### Current HL.parquet — interim format

Until the split design is agreed, `HL.parquet` is distributed as a single flat file containing all
four layers. Users should be aware of the Layer C repetition and avoid summing `TotalNumber`
across length rows without first deduplicating.

The `dr_cpue_by_haul(diag = TRUE)` function returns an approximation of the intended
`HL_catch` layer. `dr_cpue_by_length(diag = TRUE)` approximates `HL_length`.

### Derived HL columns added by {obus}

| Function | Output column | Description |
|----------|--------------|-------------|
| `dr_add_length_cm()` | `length_cm` | Length in cm (from `LengthCode` + `LengthClass`) |
| `dr_add_length_mm()` | `length_mm` | Length in mm |
| `dr_add_n_and_cpue()` | `n_haul`, `n_hour` | DataType-aware counts per haul and per hour |
| `dr_add_record_type()` | `record_type` | Integer code classifying the HL record pattern |

---

## CA — Catch-at-age

CA contains biological measurements on fish selected for ageing. One row per measured individual
(most rows) or per length class for pooled area-level samples.

CA shares the same eight haul join fields as HL. After joining on `.id`, the haul-level fields
are redundant.

### CA-specific fields (post-join)

| Field | Type | Unit | Description |
|-------|------|------|-------------|
| `ValidAphiaID` | int | — | Resolved WoRMS AphiaID |
| `AreaCode` | chr | — | Aggregation area code; check `AreaType` before joining to HH |
| `AreaType` | chr | — | `"H"` = haul-level (safe to join); other values = area-pooled |
| `FishID` | chr | — | Individual fish identifier within haul |
| `IndividualSex` | chr | — | Sex by dissection: `"M"` / `"F"` / `"U"` — distinct from `SpeciesSex` in HL |
| `LengthClass` | int | see LengthCode | Measured length of individual fish |
| `LengthCode` | chr | — | Length unit flag (see [Length Units]) |
| `LengthType` | chr | — | Total length, fork length, mantle length, etc. |
| `IndividualAge` | int | Years | Otolith-based age estimate |
| `AgePlusGroup` | chr | — | `"+"` if fish is assigned to the plus group |
| `AgeSource` | chr | — | Tissue type (otolith, scale, etc.) |
| `AgePreparationMethod` | chr | — | Preparation method |
| `OtolithGrading` | chr | — | Reader quality score |
| `IndividualWeight` | dbl | Grams | Individual body weight |
| `LiverWeight` | dbl | Grams | Liver weight when sampled |
| `IndividualMaturity` | chr | — | Maturity stage code |
| `MaturityScale` | chr | — | Maturity scale used (M6 or SMSF from 2021+) |
| `NumberAtLength` | dbl | — | Count at this length/age class (pooled rows only) |

> **Important:** `AreaCode`/`AreaType` in CA means CA rows are not always per-haul. When
> `AreaType != "H"`, age samples are pooled across multiple hauls within an area. Joining these
> rows to a specific HH record is ambiguous. Always check `AreaType` before assuming a 1:1 join.

---

---

## The DataType Field {#the-datatype-field}

`DataType` is set at the haul level (HH) and governs how `NumberAtLength` and `TotalNumber` in
HL must be interpreted. It is the single most common source of silent errors when working with
exchange data.

| DataType | Sampling protocol | `SubsamplingFactor` | `NumberAtLength` | `n_haul` formula |
|----------|-------------------|--------------------|--------------------|-----------------|
| `R` | Raw catch; some species sub-sampled | ≥ 1 | Count in sub-sample | `NumberAtLength × SubsamplingFactor` |
| `S` | Unsorted bulk sub-sample | > 1 | Count in sub-sample | `NumberAtLength × SubsamplingFactor` |
| `P` | Size-stratified (pseudocategory) | ≥ 1 per stratum | Count in sub-sample for stratum | `NumberAtLength × SubsamplingFactor` |
| `C` | Pre-raised to 1-hour haul | 1 | Numbers per hour (not a raw count) | `NumberAtLength × HaulDuration / 60` |

For DataTypes R, S, P: `n_hour = n_haul / HaulDuration × 60`  
For DataType C: `n_hour = NumberAtLength` (already per hour)

`TotalNumber` and `SpeciesCategoryWeight` are similarly DataType-dependent:

- **R/S/P:** total per-haul count and weight
- **C:** total per-hour count and weight (multiply by `HaulDuration / 60` to recover per-haul values)

`dr_add_n_and_cpue()` encodes these formulas and produces `n_haul` and `n_hour` columns that are
always per-haul and per-hour counts regardless of DataType.

---

## Unique Haul Identifier {#unique-haul-identifier}

`dr_add_id()` constructs a composite key that uniquely identifies each haul across all surveys,
years, and countries:

```
.id = Survey:Year:Quarter:Country:Platform:Gear:StationName:HaulNumber
```

Example: `"NS-IBTS:2023:1:NL:58G2:GOV:18:27"`

This is the recommended join key for all operations on the IMBUS product. A shorter 6-field key
(`.id2 = Survey:Year:Quarter:Platform:Gear:HaulNumber`) is also available for comparison with the
ICES CPUEL derived product, which omits `Country` and `StationName`.

`dr_add_id()` auto-detects legacy vs. standard column names.

---

---

## Zero Observations

A CPUE value of zero means the species was **absent** from that haul but was caught by at least
one country in the same survey/year/quarter. Zero rows are distinct from `NA` (missing data) and
are required for unbiased abundance indices.

The functions `dr_cpue_by_length(zerofill = TRUE)` and `dr_cpue_by_haul(zerofill = TRUE)` insert
explicit zero rows for all haul × species combinations where the species is absent from the haul
but present elsewhere in the survey/year/quarter.

Zero-fill adds substantial rows and forces collection into memory. It is not applied by default;
users who need it for index calculations must enable it explicitly.

---

---

## CPUE Calculation Pathways {#cpue-pathways}

Two CPUE calculation pathways are within WP2 scope as documented procedures, both fully
reproducible from the standardised exchange tables.

A third pathway — the **official ICES CPUEA product** — is accessible via
`dr_get("CPUEA", ...)` and `dr_get("IDX", ...)` but its internal age-slicing logic is determined
by ICES expert groups and is not replicated here.

### CPUE-by-length {#cpue-by-length}

The calculation requires five steps:

1. **Filter valid hauls:** retain `HaulValidity == "V"`.
2. **Filter standard species records:** retain `SpeciesValidity == "1"`.
3. **Join DataType and HaulDuration from HH to HL** on `.id`.
4. **Compute `n_haul` and `n_hour`** using the DataType-appropriate formula (see [The DataType Field]).
5. **Aggregate** across `SpeciesSex` and `SpeciesCategory` (sum `n_hour` per `.id` × `ValidAphiaID` × `length_mm`).

The `dr_cpue_by_length()` function implements this procedure in full:

```r
library(obus)
library(dplyr)

hh <- dr_con("HH") |>
  filter(Survey == "NS-IBTS", Year == 2023, Quarter == 1)
hl <- dr_con("HL") |>
  filter(Survey == "NS-IBTS", Year == 2023, Quarter == 1)

# CPUE at length (numbers per hour per haul per species)
cpue <- dr_cpue_by_length(hh, hl)

# Same with zero-fill
cpue_zf <- dr_cpue_by_length(hh, hl, zerofill = TRUE)
```

Output columns: `.id`, `Survey`, `Year`, `Quarter`, `ValidAphiaID`, `length_mm`, `n_hour`.

Zero-fill rows have `length_mm = NA` and `n_hour = 0`.

The `diag = TRUE` argument returns the pre-aggregation intermediate table for QC inspection,
retaining `SpeciesSex`, `SpeciesCategory`, and the per-row `n_haul`/`n_hour` values.

### Validation

The output of `dr_cpue_by_length()` has been validated against the official ICES DATRAS
CPUE-per-length product (CPUEL, accessible via `dr_get("CPUEL", ...)`). The procedure is
transparent and open-source, allowing independent verification of the ICES methodology and
exploration of alternative calculation choices (length filters, haul validity rules, etc.).

### Exploratory CPUE-by-age {#cpue-by-age}

The **official ICES CPUEA product** embeds survey- and species-specific decisions — which
age-length key (ALK) to use, which hauls to pool across when constructing it, how to handle plus
groups — that vary by survey, quarter, and assessment group. These decisions are not encoded in
the exchange tables and are therefore not replicable from WP2 data alone.

An **exploratory CPUE-by-age** calculation is, however, within scope and is fully achievable
from the exchange tables. The distinction is important:

| | Official CPUEA | Exploratory CPUE-by-age |
|-|---------------|------------------------|
| ALK source | ICES expert-group agreed procedure | User-defined; constructed from CA within the same survey/year/quarter |
| Pooling level | Survey/species specific (may pool across strata or quarters) | User's choice |
| Plus-group handling | Survey/species agreed convention | User's choice |
| Appropriate for | Formal stock assessment | Research, method comparison, exploratory analysis |
| Accessible via | `dr_get("CPUEA", ...)` | User pipeline on HL + CA |

The general calculation pathway is:

1. Compute CPUE-by-length from HL (as above).
2. Construct an ALK from CA for the same `.id` set or a broader pooling area.
3. Apply the ALK to the length-disaggregated CPUE to obtain numbers per hour by age.
4. Aggregate across hauls to obtain survey-level indices.

Steps 2–4 involve analytical choices not standardised in this document. The proposed intermediate
parquet products (Section [Proposed Intermediate Parquet Products]) would make step 1 trivial,
reducing the entry barrier for exploratory by-age analysis.

---

## Proposed Intermediate Parquet Products {#middle-data}

### Motivation

Working directly with the exchange tables requires knowledge of several interlocking conventions:

- `DataType` in HH governs the meaning of `TotalNumber`, `NumberAtLength`, and
  `SpeciesCategoryWeight` in HL — a variable's numeric meaning changes depending on a field
  in a different table.
- `LengthCode` in HL/CA determines the unit of `LengthClass` — a raw integer means different
  things depending on a separate flag column.
- `SubsamplingFactor`, `SpeciesCategory`, and `SpeciesSex` are distinct grouping dimensions
  in HL that must all be respected before summing; collapsing them naively produces inflated counts.
- `TotalNumber` is repeated across every length row for a given species/category group and
  must be deduplicated before use.
- CA rows may represent individual fish or area-pooled samples, depending on `AreaType`.

The result is that every research group using the exchange data must implement the same resolution
logic — often independently and with subtly different choices — before any analysis can begin.
This is the root cause of the reproducibility and comparability problems identified by survey data
users (D2.1, UR-D2, UR-Q3).

A set of **intermediate (middle-data) parquet files** would resolve these conventions once, in
one documented place, and distribute analysis-ready tables. Users who do not need access to the
raw submission format would never need to encounter DataType, LengthCode, or SubsamplingFactor
as raw fields. The raw exchange parquets remain available for users who require full fidelity.

This section presents the candidate design. It is a proposal for discussion with the IMBUS team
and the ICES Datacenter — it is not yet implemented.

---

### Proposed table: `HL_catch.parquet`

**Grain:** one row per `.id` × `aphia` × `SpeciesSex` × `DevelopmentStage` × `SpeciesCategory`

**What it resolves:** The DataType-dependent arithmetic for `TotalNumber` and
`SpeciesCategoryWeight`. These fields currently carry different semantics depending on whether the
haul was processed as DataType R/S (per-haul counts/weights) or C (per-hour counts/weights already
raised to a 60-minute standard). The intermediate table exposes only the results:

| Field | Type | Unit | Description |
|-------|------|------|-------------|
| `.id` | chr | — | Haul join key |
| `aphia` | int | — | Resolved WoRMS AphiaID |
| `SpeciesValidity` | chr | — | Record type filter (see Annex A.3) |
| `SpeciesSex` | chr | — | Population-level sex (visual) |
| `DevelopmentStage` | chr | — | Non-invasive stage code |
| `SpeciesCategory` | int | — | Subsampling category ID |
| `SubsamplingFactor` | dbl | — | Raise factor (retained for QC; not needed for `n_haul`) |
| `SubsampledNumber` | int | count | Specimens actually measured |
| `SubsampleWeight` | int | grams | Weight of measured subsample |
| `n_haul` | dbl | count | DataType-corrected total catch per haul (replaces `TotalNumber`) |
| `w_haul` | dbl | grams | DataType-corrected catch weight per haul (replaces `SpeciesCategoryWeight`) |

**What users no longer need to know:** DataType, raw `TotalNumber`, raw
`SpeciesCategoryWeight`. The `n_haul` and `w_haul` fields are always per-haul counts and weights,
regardless of how the original haul was processed.

---

### Proposed table: `HL_length.parquet`

**Grain:** one row per `.id` × `aphia` × `SpeciesSex` × `DevelopmentStage` × `SpeciesCategory` × `length_cm`

**What it resolves:** The `LengthCode`-dependent unit of `LengthClass`, and the DataType
dependency of `NumberAtLength`. The intermediate table exposes a single standardised length in cm
and a count that is always in sub-sample units (multiply by `SubsamplingFactor` for haul-level
estimate).

| Field | Type | Unit | Description |
|-------|------|------|-------------|
| `.id` | chr | — | Haul join key |
| `aphia` | int | — | Resolved WoRMS AphiaID |
| `SpeciesSex` | chr | — | Population-level sex |
| `DevelopmentStage` | chr | — | Non-invasive stage |
| `SpeciesCategory` | int | — | Subsampling category |
| `length_cm` | dbl | cm | Standardised length (from `LengthCode` + `LengthClass`) |
| `LengthType` | chr | — | Measurement type (total, fork, mantle, etc.) |
| `NumberAtLength` | dbl | count | Sub-sample count at this length class |
| `SubsamplingFactor` | dbl | — | Retained for scaling to haul-level counts |

**What users no longer need to know:** `LengthCode`, raw `LengthClass`, DataType (the
DataType-C distinction in `NumberAtLength` semantics is documented but the field is left as
sub-sample count uniformly — scaling to `n_haul` and `n_hour` is a single, obvious multiplication).

---

### Proposed table: `CA_raised.parquet` *(exploratory — requires further design)*

**Grain:** one row per `.id` × `aphia` × `age`

**What it provides:** Age-disaggregated abundance at the haul level, derived by applying an
age-length key (ALK) constructed from CA to the length-disaggregated CPUE from `HL_length`.
This is the building block for exploratory CPUE-by-age analysis.

**Design sketch:**

| Field | Type | Unit | Description |
|-------|------|------|-------------|
| `.id` | chr | — | Haul join key |
| `aphia` | int | — | Resolved WoRMS AphiaID |
| `age` | int | years | Age class (integers up to plus-group threshold) |
| `plus_group` | lgl | — | Whether this row represents the open-ended plus group |
| `n_haul` | dbl | count | Estimated numbers at age per haul (from ALK × HL catch) |
| `n_hour` | dbl | count/hr | Numbers at age per hour of hauling |
| `alk_source` | chr | — | Identifies the pooling level used to construct the ALK |

**Open questions that must be resolved before implementation:**

- **ALK pooling level:** Should the ALK be constructed per haul, per survey/year/quarter, or
  pooled across years? This is the most consequential methodological choice, and different
  surveys have established conventions.
- **Plus-group threshold:** The maximum age before collapsing into the plus group is
  species- and survey-specific. Should this be hard-coded, configurable, or left to the user?
- **CA coverage:** Not all species in HL have CA age samples in every year or quarter. Hauls
  without CA data would receive `NA` or would require borrowing from a broader ALK — both
  choices must be documented.
- **AreaType != "H" CA rows:** Some CA records are area-pooled, not haul-level (see Section 7).
  The ALK construction must handle these appropriately.
- **Sex disaggregation:** Should `CA_raised` retain `SpeciesSex`, or always aggregate across sex?

This table is more exploratory in character than `HL_catch` and `HL_length`, and its design
requires input from WP4 (which will use age-disaggregated data for index work) as well as
confirmation from Vaishav Soni (ICES) that the proposed ALK approach is compatible with the
DATRAS data architecture.

---

### Alternative: `HL_protocol.parquet` as a third split table

An earlier design proposal (see retired `table_structure.qmd`) suggested a third table:

**`HL_protocol.parquet`** — one row per `.id`, containing only `DataType` and `HaulDuration`.
The rationale was to make explicit at the HL level what sampling protocol governs a haul, rather
than requiring a join to HH. In practice, since `DataType` and `HaulDuration` already appear in
the `HH.parquet` design above, a separate `HL_protocol` table adds little unless additional
haul-level processing metadata need to be surfaced in future (e.g. mesh size used for subsampling,
deck-sorting time). It is noted here as an option but is not part of the current two-table proposal.

### Summary: proposed data tiers

```
Tier 0 — Raw exchange parquets
  HH.parquet, HL.parquet, CA.parquet, LT.parquet
  All original submission fields; legacy names translated to standard on fetch.
  For: expert users, QC, full-fidelity reproduction.

Tier 1 — Intermediate (middle-data) parquets      ← proposed
  HL_catch.parquet    DataType-corrected counts and weights by haul/species/category
  HL_length.parquet   Standardised length (cm) and sub-sample counts
  CA_raised.parquet   Age-disaggregated abundance (ALK-based; design TBD)
  For: most analytical users; no knowledge of DataType or LengthCode required.

Tier 2 — Derived products
  CPUE-by-length  dr_cpue_by_length() applied to Tier 1 or Tier 0
  CPUE-by-age     User ALK pipeline applied to Tier 1 (exploratory)
  CPUEA / IDX     Official ICES products; accessible via dr_get()
  For: downstream modelling, stock assessment, spatial analysis.
```

The Tier 1 tables do not replace the exchange parquets — they complement them. The exchange
format is the authoritative submission record; the intermediate tables are a convenience layer
that removes friction for the majority of users.

---

## Data Access

### Lazy parquet connection (recommended)

```r
library(obus)
library(dplyr)

# Connect without downloading — filter then collect
hh <- dr_con("HH") |> filter(Survey == "NS-IBTS", Year >= 2020)
hl <- dr_con("HL") |> filter(Survey == "NS-IBTS", Year >= 2020)
ca <- dr_con("CA") |> filter(Survey == "NS-IBTS", Year >= 2020)

hh_local <- collect(hh)
```

### Full download via `dr_get()`

```r
# All surveys, all years
hh <- dr_get("HH")

# Filtered via legacy XML API (slower; use parquet for large queries)
hh <- dr_get("HH", surveys = "NS-IBTS", years = 2020:2024, source = "xml")
```

### Direct parquet URLs

Any tool that reads parquet or connects to DuckDB can query these files without {obus}:

| Table | URL |
|-------|-----|
| HH | `https://heima.hafro.is/~einarhj/datras/HH.parquet` |
| HL | `https://heima.hafro.is/~einarhj/datras/HL.parquet` |
| CA | `https://heima.hafro.is/~einarhj/datras/CA.parquet` |

Python users: readable via `duckdb`, `pyarrow.parquet`, or `pandas.read_parquet`.

### Derived and supplementary products

| Product | Access | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Flex file (FL) | `dr_get("FL", ...)` | Survey-specific additional fields |
| Litter assessment (LT) | `dr_get("LT", ...)` | Marine litter per haul |
| CPUE per length (CPUEL) | `dr_get("CPUEL", ...)` | Official ICES product; ~30s per survey/year/quarter |
| CPUE per age (CPUEA) | `dr_get("CPUEA", ...)` | Age-disaggregated CPUE |
| Catch weight (CW) | `dr_get("CW", ..., aphia = ...)` | Catch weight by species |
| Survey indices (IDX) | `dr_get("IDX", ..., aphia = ...)` | Age-based survey indices |

---

## Known Limitations and Open Design Decisions

1. **HL split design requires IMBUS team input.** The current `HL.parquet` is an intermediate
   format. The end-goal split into `HL_catch` and `HL_length` parquets is proposed (Section
   \@ref(hl-split)) but cannot be finalised until the Layer B/C field gaps (development stage,
   length measurement type, sub-sample weight) are confirmed by Vaishav Soni and Adrianna Villamor
   (action A2, 1 June 2026 meeting).

2. **Parquet hosting is interim.** The current files are on a personal server
   (`heima.hafro.is`). Transfer to the ICES open access web portal (D2.4) is pending WP5/ICES
   coordination.

3. **Fast-download pathway.** Per decisions D2 and D3 from the 1 June 2026 IMBUS meeting,
   fast-download functionality (parquet via DuckDB) should be incorporated into the official
   ICES DATRAS R package rather than maintained as a separate tool. The current parquet
   infrastructure at `heima.hafro.is` is the interim implementation pending that integration.

4. **SubsamplingFactor NA.** Approximately 0.015% of HL records have `SubsamplingFactor = NA`
   under DataType R (Norwegian and Dutch vessels in NS-IBTS). This propagates as `NA` in
   `n_haul` and `n_hour`.

5. **TotalNumber rounding for DataType C.** `TotalNumber` is a back-converted CPUE count for
   DataType C; arithmetic checks reveal rounding differences of 1–20 fish (~1% of DataType C
   groups). This is an artefact of the ICES submission process and is expected.

6. **SpeciesSex vs. IndividualSex naming.** HL uses `SpeciesSex`; CA uses `IndividualSex`.
   The concepts differ (visual sex of the bulk catch vs. dissection-based individual sex), and
   the distinct names are intentional. Joining HL and CA on the sex field without accounting for
   this produces incorrect results.

7. **`AreaCode`/`AreaType` in CA.** CA rows can represent area-pooled age samples, not individual
   haul observations. Check `AreaType` before joining CA to HH.

8. **Per-survey metadata.** Concise per-survey fact sheets (coverage, gear, QC history, time-series
   breaks) are not produced by D2.2. This is addressed separately under user requirement UR-Q1.

9. **Area standardisation incomplete.** Mapping from haul positions to C-squares and ICES
   divisions is partially implemented (`dr_assign_area()`) but C-square and division lookup
   helpers are not yet in {obus}.

---

## Cross-table Ambiguities

Several fields share names or concepts across tables but differ in meaning.

**Across HL and CA:**

| Issue | HL | CA |
|-------|----|----|
| Sex field | `SpeciesSex` — visual, population-level inspection of bulk catch | `IndividualSex` — determined by dissection |
| `NumberAtLength` | Sub-sample count per length bin; multiply by `SubsamplingFactor` for population estimate | Count of aged fish at that length class; used as raising key for ALK, not a population count |
| `LengthClass` | Lower boundary of a length frequency bin (many fish share the same value) | Measured length of an individual fish |

**Gear fields duplicated across HH, HL, and CA:**

`Gear`, `GearExceptions`, `SweepLength`, and `DoorType` appear in all three tables with identical
values. This is a submission artefact: each exchange file is self-describing. Analytically, HH is
the sole source of truth. The copies in HL and CA are redundant after joining on `.id` and should
be dropped in any tidy representation.

**Within HL, DataType-dependent variables** are the most common source of silent errors
(see [The DataType Field]).

---

## Field Reference, Technical Details, and Quick Reference {#technical-reference}

Complete field reference tables (all HH, HL, CA, and LT fields with types, units, and valid
values), detailed length-unit and species-coding rules, area standardisation, data quality
checks, a drop-or-derive quick reference, and the {obus} code quick reference are in the
companion document:

> **[DATRAS Field Reference](datras_field_reference.qmd)**


## Related documents

- [DATRAS Field Reference](datras_field_reference.qmd) — companion technical reference:
  complete field tables, length units, species coding, area standardisation, QC, drop-or-derive
- `docs_external/DATRAS_Technical_Reference.md` — full DATRAS field and unit reference (ICES)
- IMBUS project website — formal deliverables, meeting notes, and project background
  (see parallel `imbus/` project)
